title: Store Distribution Architecture SSOT
compatibility_markers: user-selected/sandbox/security-scoped OS grant 와 App Review approval
decisions: Provider CLIs are not included in package artifacts; they are user-installed external tools.
Mac uses Developer ID notarized distribution first; Mac App Store requires sandbox/helper/workspace grant redesign.
Windows uses MSIX sideload first; Microsoft Store requires packaged desktop app and review evidence.
Store App and Local Helper are separated by role: UI owns consent/status/grants, helper owns local-only IPC and task execution orchestration.
Store review mode is required and must work without provider CLIs.

daemon_changes.output: Unix socket / Windows named pipe adapters behind one port
dev-local / Developer ID / App Store / MSIX path selection
macOS security-scoped bookmark / Windows folder grant representation
reviewable/offline UX without provider spawn or SaaS telemetry sync
executable check that provider binaries are not bundled

server_changes.output: daemon poll/register payload includes channel/app version/status only
task assignment excludes store-blocked or login-required runtimes
store-review-only SaaS seed/provisioning without real provider CLI
API collection scope matches the public policy metadata allowlist

review_notes: Riido does not include Claude / Codex / OpenClaw / Cursor Agent binaries.
Users connect provider CLIs they installed separately.
Provider execution requires explicit user consent and visible connected status.
Workspace access is limited to user-selected folders.
Background helper / startup behavior is opt-in and revocable.
SaaS sync does not upload provider executable paths, workspace absolute paths, provider tokens, API keys, or raw environment values.
Demo/review mode is available when provider CLIs are absent.
